
DC Circuits
- U,I = constant
Transient States
- dynamic
- U/I = constant (on/off)
- $u(t),i(t)\epsilon \mathbb{R}$
- "s"
AC Circuits
- u(t),i(t) = periodic sin/cos function
- parameters = const A(Apss),f(T),$\varphi$
- Stationary CIR.
- "s"->"jw"
---
"t": \[v]
u(t)=$U_{A}\sin(wt+\underbrace{ \varphi }_{ \text{Initial phase{[°]}} })$
Amplitude
Angular freg: $w\left[ \frac{2ad}{s} \right]$
$f=\frac{1}{T}\left[ Hz=\frac{1}{5} \right]$
$w=2\pi f=\frac{2\pi}{T}$

$U_{RMS}=\frac{U_{A}}{\sqrt{ 2 }} \ \sin/\cos$

---
$U(jw)=U_{ems}e^{j\varphi}$

---
ex.1
KVL: $e(t)=U_{R}(t)+U_{C}(t)$
$U_{R}(t)=R·i(t)$
$U_{C}(t)=\frac{1}{C}\sin(t)dt+\underbrace{ U_{0} }_{ 0V }$
$20\sin(\underbrace{ 10000 }_{ \omega }t)=R·i(t)+\frac{1}{}$

1 $''t''=''j\omega''$
R -> R
$C \to Z_{C}(j\omega)=\frac{1}{j\omega C}=-j \frac{1}{\omega C}[Ω]$
$e(t)\to E(j\omega)=E_{rms}e^{j\varphi}=\frac{\overbrace{ E_{A} }^{ 20V }}{\sqrt{ 2 }}e^{j 0°}$
$=14.1e^{j 0°}V=14.1V$

2 Solve circuit (in "$j\omega$")
$I(j\omega)=\frac{E(j\omega)}{R+Zc(j\omega)}=\frac{14.1V}{100Ω+}$

$\boxed{\text{rad=1}}$

$Z_{C}(j\omega)=-j \frac{1}{10000 \frac{rad}{\underbrace{ S }_{ 0.01S }}·1µF}$

$I(j\omega)=\frac{E(j\omega)}{R+Z_{C}(j\omega)}$

Prawo ciula ??????

1| (t)=H(t)=
$$
\begin{cases}
1  & t>0 \\
1 & t=0 \\
0 & t<0
\end{cases}
$$

Method 1 Laplace method (Laplace transform)

Method 2 Physical method (Simplified method / method of boundary values)
1. Linear DC circuit
2. Step excitation
3. I-st order circuit
 } Exponential response
 $f(t)=A+Be^{-t/\underbrace{ T }_{ const }}$
 calculate A, B and T

General equasion

$f(t)=f(\infty)+[f(0^{+})-f(\infty)]e^{-t/T}$
$T=(R||R_{in})C=3.33MΩ·15pF=50µs$
$u(t)=6.67V+[0-6.67]e^{-t/T}=$

$=6.67+(1-e^{-t/T})V \ \ t> 0$
$T=50µs$

$$
u(t)=\begin{cases}
0V & t<0 \\
6.67(1-e^{-t/T})V & t\geq 0 \\
\end{cases}
$$
$u(t)=6.67(1-e^{-t/T})·\underbrace{ 1| }_{ \text{bold 1} }(t)V \ \ t e\epsilon \mathbb{R}$

Shanns, monospace, Segoe UI Emoji" font-size="28px" fill="#1e1e1e" text-anchor="start" style="white-space: pre;" direction="ltr" dominant-baseline="alphabetic">5T t[µs]</text></g></svg>

$i(t)=0.67µA+[2-0.67]e^{ -t/T }µA=$
$=0.67+1.33e^{-t/T}µA \ t\geq 0$
$$
i(t)=
\begin{cases}
0A & t<0 \\
0.67+1.33e^{-t/T} & t\geq 0
\end{cases}
$$
$i(t)=(0.67+1.33e^{-t/T})1|(t)µA$

## Ex2
What constant current is required to charge a 1 µf capacitor from 0 to 8V in time of 9ms
i(t)=const=?
C=1µF
0->8V
$t_{0}$=9ms

17.5)"><text x="0" y="24.5" font-family="Comic Shanns, monospace, Segoe UI Emoji" font-size="28px" fill="#1e1e1e" text-anchor="start" style="white-space: pre;" direction="ltr" dominant-baseline="alphabetic">t[ms]</text></g><g transform="translate(168 924.55859375) rotate(0 53.900245666503906 17.5)"><text x="0" y="24.5" font-family="Comic Shanns, monospace, Segoe UI Emoji" font-size="28px" fill="#1e1e1e" text-anchor="start" style="white-space: pre;" direction="ltr" dominant-baseline="alphabetic">u(t)[V]</text></g><g stroke-linecap="round"><g transform="translate(68 1224.55859375) rotate(0 40 0)"><path d="M0 0 C13.33 0, 66.67 0, 80 0 M0 0 C13.33 0, 66.67 0, 80 0" stroke="#1e1e1e" stroke-width="4" fill="none"></path></g></g><mask></mask></svg>
$u(t)=\frac{1}{C}t$
$\Delta u=\frac{1}{C}\Delta t$
$u(t_{2})-u(t_{1})=\frac{1}{C}(t_{2}-t_{1})$
$I=C \frac{u(t_{2})-u(t_{1})}{t_{2}-t_{1}}=1µF \frac{8V-0V}{9ms-0ms}=1µF \frac{8V}{9ms}$
$I=\frac{8}{9}mA=0.89A$

---
## Ex.3
a constant current of 5mA charges 10µF capacitor for time of 10ms. Calculate energy which is stored in the capacitor at this period of time if initial voltage of capacitor is 2 Volts
i(t)=I=5mA=const
C=10µF
$t_{2}$=10ms
W($t_{2}$)=?
$u_{0}=2V$
$u(t)=\frac{1}{C} \int i(t)dt+u_{0}=\frac{1}{C}\int Idt+u_{0}=\frac{1}{C}\int dt+u_{0}=$
$=\frac{1}{C}t+u_{0}=\frac{1}{C}t·1|(t)+u_{0}=\frac{5mA}{10µF}·t·1|(t)+2V=$
$\frac{5}{10}=0.5k\left[ \frac{V}{s} \right]·t=0.5\left[ \frac{v}{ms} \right]$

$=0.5\left[ \frac{v}{ms} \right]t[ms]+2V$

$u(t_{2})=0.5\left[ \frac{v}{\cancel{ ms }} \right]·10\cancel{ ms }+2V=$
$=5V+2V=7V$

$W(t_{2})=\frac{1}{2}Cu^{2}(t_{2})=\frac{1}{2}·10µF·(7V)^{2}=245µJ$

# 19.01.26
Transcient states

$$
i(t)=\begin{cases}
12(1-e^{-t/T})A \\
0A
\end{cases}
$$
$i(t)=12(1-e^{-t/T})·1|(t)A$
$i(t_{0})=0.5\cancel{ imax }=\underbrace{ \cancel{ imax } }_{ i(\infty) }·(1-e^{-t_{0}/T})·1$
$0.5=1-e$
$e^{-t_{0}/T}=0.5 \ | \ln x$
$-\frac{t_{0}}{T}=\ln 0.5$
$t_{0}=-T·\ln 0.5=$
$=-0.69·T=$
$=0.69·1.38s$

$U(s) \frac{\frac{1}{sC_{1}}}{\frac{1}{sC_{2}} \frac{1}{sC_{1}}}$

the equelibrium when both capacitors will have the same voltage

S RLC
$R_{crit}=2\sqrt{ \frac{L}{C} }=2\sqrt{ \frac{6H}{7µF} }=2\sqrt{ \frac{6}{7}·1000Ω }= 1851Ω$

$0.4kΩ<1851Ω$
underdumped

$\omega = \frac{1}{\sqrt{ LC }}$ function of free oscill.

$\omega=2\pi·f$

$\alpha=\frac{R}{2L}$ -współczynik tłumienia

$\omega=\sqrt{ \omega_{0}^{2} -\alpha^{2}}=2\pi f$
$T=\frac{1}{f}$

$R_{crit}=\frac{1}{2}\sqrt{ \frac{L}{C} }$

R to $R_{crit}$ relation are inverted

$\omega_{0}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{ LC }}$
$\omega=\sqrt{ \omega_{0}^{2}-\alpha^{2} }=2\pi F$
$\alpha=\frac{1}{2RC}$
$T=\frac{1}{f}=\dots$

---
## Ex.3
$i_{1}(t_{0})=?$
$t_{0}=2ms$
$C_{1}=1µF$
$C_{2}=3µF$

Laplace Method
### 1 
"t" -> "s"
 R  ->  R 
 $E·\mathbb{1}(t)\to \frac{E}{s}$
 $C \to z(s)=\frac{1}{sC}$
 $\alpha f(t)\to\alpha F(s)$

### 2 solve the circuit
$i(s)=\frac{\frac{E}{S}}{R+z(s)}$=$\frac{\frac{E}{S}}{R+\frac{1}{S(C_{1}+C_{2})}}$=$\frac{E(C_{1}+C_{2})}{SR(C_{1}+C_{2})+1}$

$\frac{1}{Z}=\frac{1}{Z_{1}}+\frac{1}{Z_{2}}=\frac{1}{\frac{1}{SC_{1}}}+\frac{1}{\frac{1}{SC_{2}}}=$
$=sC_{1}+sC_{2}=s(C_{1}+C_{2})=$
$Z(s)=\frac{1}{S(\underbrace{ C_{1}+C_{2} }_{ C_{x} })}$
$I_{1}(s)=I(s) \frac{\frac{1}{\cancel{ s }C_{2}}}{\frac{1}{\cancel{ s }C_{1}}+\frac{1}{\cancel{ s }C_{2}}}$=$I(s) \frac{C_{1}}{C_{2}+C_{1}}$

$I_{1}(s)=\frac{C_{1}}{C_{1}+C_{2}}I(s)$
$i_{1}(t)=\frac{C_{1}}{C_{1}+C_{2}}i(t)$

pojemnościowy dzielnik prądu f>0

$I_{1}(s)=\frac{C_{1}}{\cancel{ C_{1}+C_{2} }} \frac{E\cancel{ (C_{1}+C_{2}) }}{sR(C_{1}+C_{2})}+1$=$\frac{EC_{1}}{s\underbrace{ R(C_{1}+C_{2}) }_{ T }+1}$

### 3
"s"->"t"
$\frac{T}{sT+1}=e^{-t/T}$

$\frac{1}{s(sT+1)}=1-e^{-t/T}$

$T=R(C_{1}+C_{2})=100Ω·4µF=400µs=0.4ms$
$I_{1}(s)=\frac{1}{T}· \frac{EC_{1}·T}{sT+1}$=$\frac{EC_{1}}{T}·\frac{T}{sT+1}$=
 = $\underbrace{ \frac{E}{R}·\frac{C_{1}}{C_{1}+C_{2}} }_{ cons\tan ts }·\frac{T}{sT+1}$
 $i_{1}(t)=\frac{E}{R}·\frac{C_{1}}{C_{1}+C_{2}}·e^{ -t/T }$

$i_{1}(t_{0})=\frac{E}{R}·\frac{C_{1}}{C_{1}+C_{2}} e^{ -t_{0}/T }$=
=$\frac{10V}{100Ω}·\frac{1µF}{4µF}e^{-2ms/0.4ms}$=
=$0.1·\frac{1}{4}e^{ -5 }=168µA$

## Ex 4
Ramp voltage (rises linearly)
$6 \frac{V}{s} Rise$
RC diff
$u(4T)=?$

$U(s)=E(s) \frac{R}{\frac{1}{SC}+R}$=$E(s) \frac{sRC}{1+sRC}$=$6\left[ \frac{V}{s} \right]·\frac{1}{s\cancel{ ^{2} }} \frac{\cancel{ s }RC}{sRC+1}$=
=$6\left[ \frac{V}{s} \right]·RC \underbrace{ \frac{1}{s(sRC+1)} }_{  }$
$u(t)=6\left[ \frac{V}{s} \right]·T·(1-e^{ -t/T })$
$u(4T)=6\left[ \frac{mV}{\cancel{ ms }} \right]·4\cancel{ ms }·(1-e^{-4T/T})$=$24mV·(1-e^{ -4 })=23.54mV$
